Diet_Chart_Sanjay_Sharma_Prameha_page-00: OPD TIMING: MONDAY TO SUNDAY | 10:00 AM To 7:00 PM
Condition: Prameha (Diabetes Mellitus – T2DM) | Prakriti: Kapha-Pitta | Season: Grishma Ritu (Summer) | Date: 13 April 2026
Prameha (Diabetes Mellitus) is a Kapha-Pitta disorder involving vitiation of Meda Dhatu and Kleda (fluid dysmetabolism). Diet
should focus on Rooksha (drying), Laghu (light), and Tikta-Katu-Kashaya Rasa foods that reduce Kapha and Ama.
Eat light, easily digestible foods. Avoid Guru (heavy) items.
Prefer dry, rough-textured grains like barley (Yava). Reduce oily foods.
All meals must be low-Gl. No refined flour, sugar, white rice, or sweets.
Eat at fixed times. Never skip meals — creates Vata imbalance worsening glucose control.
Avoid frequent eating — allows Agni to reset fully between meals.
Barley upma / Jowar roti (2) + vegetable sabzi
•2 jowar/bajra roti + 1 cup sabzi + dal + salad
2 roti + light sabzi (bottle gourd, bitter gourd, drumstick)• Heavy meals after 8 PM

Lifestyle Principles for Long-Term Prameha Management
A Note on Healing Prameha Through Ayurveda
Prameha is a Santarpana Janya Vyadhi (disease of excess). Reducing Kapha through diet, exercise and lifestyle
correction is the most powerful intervention. No sugar. Daily walk. Fixed eating times. Consistency is the medicine.
